payload.text:
Sto lavorando al progetto UniParty. Devo aggiungere una funzionalità di "Account Staff Temporaneo". IMPORTANTE: Questa modifica deve essere strettamente additiva. Non rimuovere, rompere o modificare le funzionalità esistenti per l'utente "Associazione" o "Studente". Il comportamento attuale deve rimanere identico, aggiungendo solo la possibilità per un nuovo ruolo "staff" di accedere allo Scanner.

Segui queste istruzioni file per file:

1. server/models.js (Database)

Azione: Mantieni tutto il codice esistente.

Modifica: Nell'enum del campo role, aggiungi la stringa 'staff'.

Aggiunta: Aggiungi il campo opzionale parentOrganization: { type: mongoose.Schema.Types.ObjectId, ref: 'User' }. Non toccare gli altri campi.

2. types.ts (TypeScript Defs)

Azione: Estendi i tipi esistenti.

Modifica: Aggiungi STAFF = 'staff' all'enum UserRole.

Aggiunta: Aggiungi parentOrganization?: string all'interfaccia User.

3. server/server.js (Backend)

Nuova Rotta: Aggiungi POST /api/auth/staff-account alla fine della sezione Auth. Questa rotta permette all'associazione di creare/aggiornare l'utente staff.

Modifica Rotta POST /api/tickets/validate:

NON CANCELLARE la logica attuale.

Attualmente la rotta controlla: if (orgId !== req.user.userId) return 403.

Cambia SOLO la condizione if: trasformala per accettare l'accesso SE orgId === req.user.userId (logica esistente) OPPURE SE req.user.role === 'staff' E req.user.parentOrganization coincide con orgId.

4. pages/Dashboard.tsx (Frontend)

Azione: Non modificare la dashboard esistente.

Aggiunta: Aggiungi semplicemente un nuovo componente o div (chiamato "Gestione Staff") in fondo alla pagina o in una nuova tab, visibile solo se l'utente è associazione. Questo box deve contenere il form per creare lo staff (chiama /api/auth/staff-account).

5. pages/Scanner.tsx (Frontend)

Logica di Accesso: Nel useEffect iniziale, dove controlli user.role, estendi la condizione per accettare ANCHE UserRole.STAFF. Non rimuovere il permesso per ASSOCIAZIONE.

Logica di Validazione (handleValidation):

Attualmente c'è un controllo lato client: if (orgId !== user._id) { error... }.

Modifica: Rilassa questo controllo. Deve dare errore solo se: orgId !== user._id E (user.role !== 'staff' || user.parentOrganization !== orgId).

6. components/Navbar.tsx (Frontend)

Azione: Non cancellare la navbar attuale.

Logica: Aggiungi un controllo condizionale all'inizio.

if (user?.role === 'staff') { return <NavbarSemplificata ... /> }

return ( ... codice originale della navbar ... )

In questo modo, l'utente normale vede esattamente quello che vedeva prima.

Generami il codice necessario applicando queste regole di conservazione.

**Adding Temporary Accounts**

I've been focusing on integrating the "Temporary Staff Account" feature into the UniParty app. The approach is additive, avoiding disruption to existing user roles like `Associazione`, `Studente`, and `Admin`. The core changes involve adding a `staff` option to the user role enum and introducing a `parentOrganization` field.


**Detailing Staff Account Logic**

I've been working on refining the implementation details. I've now added the `staff` role to the user enum and the `parentOrganization` field. Next, I'll integrate a new `auth.createStaffAccount` method. Finally, I'm adjusting the ticket validation to accommodate staff scanning for their parent organization. The goal is to make staff creation seamless from the Association user experience, and the login experience as well.


**Defining Staff Dashboard Elements**

I'm now integrating the "Gestione Staff" section into the `Dashboard.tsx` file, alongside the form fields for email and password. I'm also refining role-based access; staff will only require Scanner and Logout functionality. Finally, I'm reviewing the user access checks within the `useEffect` and `handleValidation` hooks.


**Constructing Staff Endpoint**

I'm currently working on the `/api/auth/staff-account` endpoint. It will verify that the user's role is 'associazione' before proceeding. Subsequently, it will find or establish a user with the 'staff' role, setting the `parentOrganization` to the requesting user's ID. I've also updated the `validateTicket` function to allow ticket scanning by staff members who are affiliated with the correct organization. The user interface will see a new tab for "staff," and a form to specify staff email and password. Also I am implementing the `STAFF` role, which will impact the `handleValidation` check and show limited links based on the user's role. Finally, I will adjust `BottomNav.tsx` to handle the `STAFF` role and enable the scanner link when appropriate. I will move on to drafting the XML.

entries.description: Added STAFF to UserRole enum and parentOrganization field to User interface.
Added staff role and parentOrganization field to User model.
Added staff-account management route and updated ticket validation logic for staff.
Added Staff Management section for associations to create and update staff accounts.
Updated scanner access and validation logic to support the new STAFF role.
Added a simplified navbar for the new STAFF role, showing only Scanner and Logout options.
Updated BottomNav to show only relevant icons (Home, Scanner, Logout) for the STAFF role.

diffs.replacement:
import React from 'react'; import { Link, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'; import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext'; import { UserRole } from '../types'; import { Home, Ticket, Heart, User, HelpCircle, Plus, ScanLine, LayoutDashboard, Users, Shield, Calendar, LogIn, Search, LogOut } from 'lucide-react'; const BottomNav: React.FC = () => { const { user, logout } = useAuth(); const location = useLocation(); const isActive = (path: string) => { const [pathPart, queryPart] = path.split('?'); const matchesPath = location.pathname === pathPart; if (!queryPart) return matchesPath; return matchesPath && location.search.includes(queryPart); }; return (
{/* ========================================== RUOLO: STAFF (NEW) ========================================== */} {user && user.role === UserRole.STAFF && ( <> Home {/* CENTRALE - SCANNER */}
)} {/* ========================================== GUEST ========================================== */} {!user && ( <> Home
Supporto )} {/* ========================================== RUOLO: STUDENTE ========================================== */} {user && user.role === UserRole.STUDENTE && ( <> Wallet Preferiti
Cerca Profilo )} {/* ========================================== RUOLO: ASSOCIAZIONE ========================================== */} {user && user.role === UserRole.ASSOCIAZIONE && ( <> Home Scan
Dash Profilo )} {/* ========================================== RUOLO: ADMIN ========================================== */} {user && user.role === UserRole.ADMIN && ( <> Home Utenti
Eventi Profilo )}
); }; export default BottomNav;

payload.text:
Ho implementato la creazione degli account Staff. Ora devo permettere all'Associazione di vedere la lista degli account staff che ha creato e di poterli eliminare se necessario.

Per favore, aggiungi queste funzionalità file per file:

1. server/server.js (Backend)

Nuova Rotta GET: Aggiungi GET /api/auth/staff-accounts.

Protezione: authMiddleware.

Permesso: Solo ruolo 'associazione'.

Logica: Restituisci tutti gli utenti che hanno role: 'staff' e parentOrganization: req.user.userId.

Nuova Rotta DELETE: Aggiungi DELETE /api/auth/staff-accounts/:id.

Protezione: authMiddleware.

Permesso: Solo ruolo 'associazione'.

Logica: Cerca ed elimina l'utente che ha _id: req.params.id E parentOrganization: req.user.userId. (È fondamentale verificare il parentOrganization per sicurezza, affinché un'associazione non possa cancellare lo staff di un'altra).

2. services/api.ts (Frontend Service)

Nell'oggetto auth (o dove appropriato), aggiungi due nuovi metodi:

getStaffAccounts(): chiama GET /auth/staff-accounts.

deleteStaffAccount(id: string): chiama DELETE /auth/staff-accounts/${id}.

3. pages/Dashboard.tsx (Frontend)

Stato: Aggiungi uno stato per memorizzare la lista staff (es. const [staffList, setStaffList] = useState([]);).

Caricamento: Usa un useEffect (o aggiorna quello esistente) per chiamare api.auth.getStaffAccounts() quando il componente viene caricato, salvando i dati nello stato.

UI Lista: Nella sezione "Gestione Staff" che hai creato prima, aggiungi una tabella o una lista sotto il form di creazione.

Per ogni membro dello staff mostra: Email (o nome utente).

Accanto, metti un bottone "Elimina" (icona cestino o testo rosso).

Azione Eliminazione: Al click su "Elimina", chiama api.auth.deleteStaffAccount(id), e poi aggiorna la lista locale rimuovendo l'elemento eliminato (o ricaricando la lista).

Generami il codice aggiornato per questi file. non modificare altro

**Considering Staff Access**

I'm currently focused on implementing staff account management within the UNIPArty app, starting with the backend. My first step is designing a secure API endpoint that will retrieve staff accounts. The goal is a GET request to `/api/auth/staff-accounts` that fetches all staff accounts tied to the association currently logged in.


**Expanding API Functionality**

I've just added the two new routes to `server/server.js`: `GET /api/auth/staff-accounts` and `DELETE /api/auth/staff-accounts/:id`.  Now, I'm integrating these new endpoints into the frontend by updating `services/api.ts` with corresponding methods, `auth.getStaffAccounts` and `auth.deleteStaffAccount`.  Finally, I'll move on to updating `pages/Dashboard.tsx` to handle the display of staff accounts and implementing the deletion functionality.


**Adding UI Components**

My current focus is integrating the backend API with the frontend in `Dashboard.tsx`.  I'm adding a staff list UI, including a delete button for each entry. I'm leveraging `useEffect` to fetch the staff list when the "staff" tab is active.  I'm also double-checking and updating both the `mockApi` and `realApi` methods in `api.ts` for consistency with new GET and DELETE endpoints. Adding the new route to `server/server.js` is ongoing.


**Integrating Backend Logic**

I've completed the backend implementations for retrieving and deleting staff accounts. The `GET /api/auth/staff-accounts` route now retrieves staff records, and the `DELETE` route handles account removal, both with appropriate error handling and access control. I'm updating `services/api.ts` with new `getStaffAccounts` and `deleteStaffAccount` methods.


**Completing the Integration**

I'm finishing up the frontend integration, adding the staff list display in `Dashboard.tsx`. I have set up state variables for `staffList` and `loadingStaff`. I've implemented `useEffect` to fetch and display the staff list when the "staff" tab is active and the user has association level access. I'm also adding a delete button to each staff member entry, with a confirmation prompt. The `handleDeleteStaff` function will then call the backend API.


**Refreshing Staff List**

I've just added a refresh for the staff list after a new account is created. I updated the `handleManageStaff` function so it fetches the staff list again after a successful account creation, ensuring the UI reflects the most up-to-date data. Also, the staff list UI now displays a loading message while data is being fetched, and the current display reflects active staff members.


**Improving Staff Display**

My current focus is polishing the staff list display in `Dashboard.tsx`. I've added a visual improvement, using a `map` function to render each staff member with their email and creation date.  A user icon is now displayed for each entry, and I'm ensuring the date formatting is consistent. I'm also adding a hover effect to the staff list items for better UX. I'm using a `className` for visual grouping and improved layout.


**Enhancing Staff Listing**

I'm now refining the staff list display. I'm swapping the `User` icon for the `Users` icon for consistency, and verifying all the necessary icons are available. I will next add a visual indicator if the staff list is empty, and integrate error handling to improve the user experience.


**Refining Icon Usage**

I'm currently working to integrate the Trash2 icon into `Dashboard.tsx`, ensuring consistent import practices with other icons. My existing `useEffect` logic now encompasses a new one, triggered when the `activeTab` changes. I also need to update the `mockApi` and `realApi` in `api.ts` to include the appropriate new methods. I'm focusing on ensuring all backend routes in `server/server.js` and frontend calls are correct.


**Evaluating Code Updates**

I'm now focusing on a thorough review of the code changes, ensuring they are well-structured and efficient. I am specifically checking the `useEffect` hook to ensure it correctly fetches the staff list on tab change and the newly added code for integrating the Trash2 icon. I'm also ensuring the error handling mechanisms function properly.

entries.description: Added GET and DELETE routes for managing staff accounts.
Added getStaffAccounts and deleteStaffAccount service methods.
Implemented staff list fetching and deletion in the Staff management tab.
